purpose: Define the learning goals an automated training controller should work through before promoting sequencing knowledge.

qualityTargetModel.principle: Effect, model, submodel, and layer evidence are prerequisite capability evidence. Human-level sequencing quality is promoted from section-level and full-sequence whole-display renders.
qualityTargetModel.conflictResolution: When lower-level capability evidence conflicts with section-level or full-sequence whole-display evidence, the broader rendered context wins.
qualityTargetModel.singleEffectPromotionLimit: A strong single-effect result can promote an effect capability prior only. It cannot promote creative sequencing behavior without composition, section, or full-sequence evidence.
qualityTargetModel.designRangePolicy: Scores are interpreted against the selected song section, style, model role, and design intent. Brightness, density, color richness, motion, negative space, and neutrality are valid only when they fit that intent range.
qualityTargetModel.diversityPolicy: Preserve multiple valid candidates per intent and style when they differ meaningfully in effect family, target choice, timing, palette, motion, density, or focal path.

evidenceHierarchy.description: One effect on one target or submodel. Learns visibility, motion type, fill, texture, color behavior, setting sensitivity, and geometry fit.
Multiple layers on the same target. Learns blend behavior, layer order, brightness hierarchy, fades, and timing overlap.
Multiple targets, groups, or submodels in one design window. Learns whether selected elements read coherently together.
All selected models, layers, and effects for a song section. Scores intent fit, style fit, energy range, presence, focal hierarchy, color story, motion variety, transition quality, and musical fit.

The complete sequence or representative multi-section excerpt. Scores motifs, development, repeated-section variation, energy progression, lyric and feature moments, color progression, finale payoff, and non-repetition.

areas.purpose: Prove effects can render, produce visible output, and expose useful setting regions before relying on them creatively.
Learn each effect's visible behavior, palette handling, model fit, and high-impact parameters.
Learn which effects read well across built-in, imported, and custom model structures.
Learn how same-target layers, group/member layers, order, blend, brightness, contrast, fade, and timing overlap affect display readability.
Learn parent versus submodel targeting and sibling submodel behavior through the same framework for every model type.
Learn whole-display coverage, balance, foreground/background separation, regional variety, and visual readability.
Learn section energy, phrase/beat alignment, repetition with variation, and lyric/accent timing behavior.
Learn whether generated sequences match user intent for mood, palette, pace, emphasis, and creative accuracy.

goals.description: Promote stable same-target layer-stack priors in mono-white on the vendor fixture.
nextSelectionHints: Repeat blocked high-quality same-target stack passes from the latest run.
Prioritize three_layer_default, reversed_layer_order, and setting variants with single_run_baseline status.

goals.description: Promote initial group/member layer-composition priors in mono-white on the vendor fixture.
Repeat the promoted mono-white layer-composition families under rgb_primary palette conditions.
nextSelectionHints: Start with group_then_model and same-target one/two-layer stack repeats.
Compare color spread and multicolor frame ratio against mono-white priors.

goals.description: Learn parent versus submodel behavior on vendor custom and built-in targets using shared target/submodel metadata.
nextSelectionHints: Use display/model-index.json submodel structure rather than name inference.
Probe parent, important submodel, and sibling-pair cases separately.

goals.description: Establish core effect fit across common model families before broadening the effect set.
Evaluate generated section and whole-sequence output for display-level quality rather than isolated effect success.
Repeat and stabilize selector-ready focal consistency video aesthetic evidence so the successful display-level strategy becomes durable across runs.
Repeat and stabilize RGB-primary regional-focus display review evidence so color-aware display composition becomes durable.
Learn a constrained RGB display-level strategy that preserves color discipline and visual balance by using mono-white structure with limited primary-color accents.
Learn bounded local and layer evidence inside a whole-display review pass so detailed model activity can be evaluated without sacrificing display-level balance.
nextSelectionHints: Use restrained, short-window local accents over a stable display foundation so the review can separate useful model-level detail from display-level clutter.
Prefer explicit layer intent and color-purpose metadata over model-name assumptions when describing why a local placement exists.

goals.description: Stabilize RGB-primary palette section pacing after color discipline and musical structure are covered, using whole-display review evidence that balances pacing, motion interest, and quality consistency.
nextSelectionHints: Preserve the successful RGB color-purpose discipline while testing section-level pacing variation.
Avoid broad display widening unless the video score shows visual balance is the primary remaining weakness.

goals.description: After repeated display-target regressions, validate a guarded motion target that preserves the stable palette/spatial/focal foundation before adding only one restrained motion layer.
Refine the guarded three-window display candidate by strengthening the focal hold and adding slightly clearer lift/release pacing while preserving the stable foundation.
Refine the guarded three-window display candidate by preserving the original focal anchor and reducing support-motion competition around it.
Refine the stable guarded foundation by restoring motion interest with short low-intensity counterpoint support that does not compete with the focal star.
Learn a follow-on RGB palette pacing variant that raises motion interest and pacing variety while preserving the stable palette/spatial/focal foundation.
Learn RGB display balance that uses intentional negative space and restrained focal support instead of filling more models whenever balance is weak.
Repeat the successful RGB motion pacing structure under a new pass key so the runner can collect additional durable evidence without re-entering the regressing transition bridge path.
Repeat the successful RGB spatial negative-space structure under a new pass key to collect additional stable balance evidence for selector use.
Collect additional RGB motion pacing evidence under a holdout pass key using the successful restrained section pacing structure.
Validate whether restrained RGB motion pacing remains stable when the display adds a low-intensity depth foundation.
Collect holdout evidence for RGB negative-space display balance without introducing broad fill or competing focal layers.
Validate RGB spatial focus with a sparse structural base, one focal accent, and a restrained line thread.
Validate RGB color-purpose contrast using the disciplined structure, focal accent, and motion roles learned earlier.
Validate RGB color-purpose discipline when motion support carries more of the sequence energy.
Revalidate the stable RGB motion pacing pattern under an independent validation pass key before promoting it as a durable selector prior.
Revalidate the stable RGB negative-space structure with a separate pass key for durable whole-display balance evidence.
Revalidate sparse RGB focal structure with restrained motion support and no broad fill.
Revalidate the non-regressing RGB color-purpose motion structure with a separate pass key.
Validate a constrained RGB display structure that protects focal handoff and full-sequence context after palette discipline and motion support have stabilized.
nextSelectionHints: Hold the primary focal element longer, add only one low-competition handoff thread, and keep late support as context rather than competing motion.
Prefer preserving focal handoff and full-sequence context over additional color-purpose motion.

goals.description: Validate focal handoff as a compact three-window intro/lift/release sequence so whole-sequence context is measured without dragging in a long dependency chain.
nextSelectionHints: Measure the handoff as a compact sequence: establish focal anchor, introduce one restrained bridge, then add quiet release context.
Do not add broad palette or motion support until focal handoff and context are preserved.

goals.description: Revalidate the current best guarded foundation structure under fresh pass keys before adding more sequence complexity.
nextSelectionHints: Use the stable guarded foundation as the control branch: preserve left structure, right counterweight, center focal accent, restrained line motion, and late star support.
Treat this as a reproducibility checkpoint before exploring additional complexity.

goals.description: Validate focal handoff while preserving the stable guarded foundation context so the sequence does not lose intent match, local readability, visual balance, or full-sequence context.
nextSelectionHints: Preserve the stable left/right context and focal hold before adding the restrained handoff bridge.
Use this as the repair branch after the sparse focal-handoff sequence regressed intent match, readability, and full-sequence context.

goals.description: Test a softer late counterpoint over the stable guarded foundation to improve motion and balance without repeating the focal-handoff regression from the stronger counterpoint branch.
nextSelectionHints: Preserve the stable guarded foundation, then add only a late low-brightness counterpoint so visual balance can improve without stealing the focal handoff.
Treat any focal handoff or intent-match regression as more important than small motion-interest gains.

goals.description: Test a calibrated late counterpoint between the invisible soft branch and the stronger branch that hurt focal handoff.
nextSelectionHints: Use a measurable but late counterpoint: it should improve motion and visual balance while staying below the focal star and line motion.
If focal handoff drops, retreat to the soft branch; if no metrics move, increase counterpoint presence only in a future bounded branch.

goals.description: Learn RGB transition motion bridges that connect sections while preserving palette harmony and temporal continuity.
Expand effect/model fit beyond the first core baseline using RGB-primary coverage on the active vendor fixture model families.
nextSelectionHints: Use only model keys present in the active model catalog so this remains runnable on the current training fixture.
Prefer RGB-primary runs to broaden color behavior after the mono-white core effect baseline.

goals.description: Learn how generated effects align with section energy, beat/phrase timing, lyrics, and accents.
Learn multi-section musical structure beyond isolated section response, including energy arc, motif reprise with variation, and lyric phrase release.
nextSelectionHints: Use RGB-primary because future sequencing must learn musical structure and color together.
Probe energy arc, motif reprise/variation, and lyric phrase release as separate pass IDs before promoting shared behavior.

goals.description: Blend the stable guarded display foundation with section energy, motif lift, and lyric release timing so the sequence learns musical structure without losing whole-display readability.
nextSelectionHints: Use the stable guarded foundation as the visual base, then vary timing roles by section, motif lift, and lyric release.
This branch should teach sequence-level musical phrasing rather than another display-only counterpoint variant.

goals.description: Use the stable guarded visual foundation with a clearer focal hold and measured phrase lift to recover motion/palette purpose while preserving section, beat, lyric, and accent timing.
nextSelectionHints: Preserve the guarded foundation, but use the clearer focal-hold and measured line lift that previously produced stable display evidence.
This branch should retain music timing coverage while repairing the guarded music branch's weak motion and palette-purpose movement.

goals.description: Train full-sequence song response using intro, build, and release sections with audio-intent labels so generated sequences can learn energy arc, beat/motif continuity, lyric release, and whole-display payoff together.
nextSelectionHints: Treat this as full-sequence song response training: intro establishes structure, build introduces motif and motion, release creates focal payoff.
Promote only when the whole-display video remains readable and the music review shows section, beat, phrase, lyric, and accent timing coverage.

goals.description: Repair full-sequence audio alignment by preserving intro/build/release intent while reducing color-role churn, abrupt motion shifts, and section-to-section inconsistency.
nextSelectionHints: Keep the audio section roles from the first audio-alignment branch, but use a more consistent palette and gentler motion handoff.
This repair is targeted at quality consistency, color discipline, temporal continuity, and full-sequence context.

goals.description: Repair broad full-sequence audio alignment by keeping the guarded visual foundation and adding only single-target phrase and release cues so focal clarity gains do not cost color discipline, quality consistency, temporal continuity, or full-sequence context.
nextSelectionHints: Use this instead of retrying the broad audio alignment branch after quality-consistency and color-discipline regressions.
Preserve the guarded structure and keep timing cues on one compatible support target.

goals.description: Layer synthesized intro, phrase, lyric, and release timing cues over the strongest guarded visual foundation without rebuilding the whole-display structure.
nextSelectionHints: Preserve the guarded display foundation and add only subtle synthetic timing cues.
This branch should test whether music labels can improve intent and section quality while keeping whole-display quality near the best baseline.

goals.description: Repair the baseline-preserving synthesized audio overlay by adding only minimal motion/color-purpose support while preserving the guarded display foundation.
nextSelectionHints: Use the close baseline-preserving overlay as the base, then add only low-brightness motion support to repair motion interest and palette purpose.
Avoid changing focal hierarchy, display order, or section structure.

goals.description: Repair the close baseline-preserving synthesized audio overlay by assigning the release cue a distinct lyric focal accent purpose without adding new visual layers.
nextSelectionHints: Keep the same visible overlay as the close baseline-preserving candidate.
Only distinguish the lyric release color purpose so palette-purpose coverage is explicit without adding display clutter.

goals.description: Repair motion interest in the promoted baseline-preserving synthesized audio overlay by increasing the existing phrase overlay's motion contrast without adding layers or changing focal hierarchy.
nextSelectionHints: Use the promoted palette-role overlay as the base.
Increase motion range only by retiming the existing phrase overlay; do not add a new prop or competing focal layer.

goals.description: Validate a second baseline-preserving synthesized audio overlay style that keeps the promoted display foundation and semantic timing roles while varying phrase/release timing and color-purpose emphasis.
nextSelectionHints: Use the promoted palette-role overlay as the reference foundation.
Explore a controlled timing/style variation that preserves whole-display hierarchy instead of optimizing toward one static effect recipe.

goals.description: Validate a baseline-preserving synthesized audio call/response style that keeps the guarded display foundation while alternating restrained phrase and lyric accents across compatible targets.
nextSelectionHints: Use the promoted guarded foundation and style-variation evidence as the reference.
Test restrained call/response timing across line and focal targets without adding broad display activity.

goals.description: Validate a baseline-preserving synthesized audio motif variation that keeps phrase, reprise, and release echo on one support target so the established focal handoff is not disturbed.
nextSelectionHints: Use the promoted guarded foundation and style-variation evidence as the reference.
Keep all added motif timing on one support target to test style range without cross-target focal competition.

goals.description: Validate a baseline-preserving synthesized section-energy swell that keeps all added timing on one support target while changing intensity across intro, phrase, and release windows.
nextSelectionHints: Use the promoted guarded foundation and single-target motif evidence as the reference.
Explore a gradual support-target energy swell without moving attention away from the established focal handoff.

goals.description: Validate a sparse baseline-preserving synthesized accent style that keeps only one short support-target phrase cue and one restrained release cue over the guarded foundation.
nextSelectionHints: Use the promoted guarded foundation and single-target overlay evidence as the reference.
Reduce accent density while preserving a readable phrase cue and release cue on the same support target.

goals.description: Validate a sparse baseline-preserving synthesized accent style that keeps density and timing stable while changing the support phrase to a secondary palette accent role.
nextSelectionHints: Use the promoted sparse-accent branch as the reference.
Keep sparse timing and target selection stable while testing whether a secondary accent purpose adds useful palette range.

goals.description: Repair the failed sparse secondary-palette overlay by preserving the same sparse timing and support target while returning the phrase cue to the disciplined cool-motion accent role.
nextSelectionHints: Use this instead of retrying the sparse secondary-palette branch after color-discipline and palette-purpose regressions.
Keep sparse timing and target selection stable, but do not activate the reserved secondary accent color.

goals.description: Validate a sparse baseline-preserving synthesized accent style that keeps target and color-purpose stable while delaying phrase and release accents for a later emphasis contour.
nextSelectionHints: Use the promoted sparse-accent branch as the reference.
Keep sparse density, target selection, and color purpose stable while shifting accent timing later.

goals.description: Validate a sparse baseline-preserving synthesized accent style that keeps target and color-purpose stable while moving phrase and release accents earlier for a front-weighted emphasis contour.
nextSelectionHints: Use the promoted sparse-delayed branch as the reference.
Keep sparse density, target selection, and color purpose stable while shifting accent timing earlier.

goals.description: Validate a sparse baseline-preserving synthesized syncopated echo style that preserves target and color-purpose discipline while adding a second low-brightness offbeat phrase echo.
nextSelectionHints: Use the promoted sparse-delayed and sparse-early branches as timing-range references.
Keep target selection and color purposes stable while testing a restrained offbeat phrase echo.

goals.description: Validate a sparse baseline-preserving synthesized release-hold style that keeps the phrase cue restrained and stretches the lyric release cue without adding targets.
nextSelectionHints: Use the promoted sparse-delayed branch as the reference for late emphasis.
Keep the same support target and semantic color roles while testing whether a restrained release hold adds useful style range.

goals.description: Measure whether generated sequences match user creative intent beyond mechanical rendering.
blockedBy: needs display-level and musical-structure evidence first

goals.description: Measure whether targeted revisions improve creative intent match compared with the original generated section.
nextSelectionHints: Generate a baseline creative intent pass and a targeted revision pass with comparisonBasePassId linking the pair.
Score the revision by overall quality delta, intent match delta, readability delta, and whether the change fixed the declared issue without adding clutter.

goals.description: Compare targeted creative revision variants so the selector can learn when focus simplification, focal handoff stabilization, or pacing balance improves intent match and video-level readability.
nextSelectionHints: Compare the same first draft against focus-simplification, focal-handoff-stability, and pacing-balance revisions.
Promote only if the targeted objective improves without clutter, readability, or motion-coherence regression.

goals.description: Validate that shared baseline priors transfer only through compatible or similar target structure, while structurally weak matches are treated as project-local validation probes.
nextSelectionHints: Use shared-prior-compatible arch evidence as the source context.
Probe a similar linear cane target as advisory transfer evidence.
Probe a structurally different matrix target as local-validation-required evidence, not confident shared-prior reuse.
